differential_diagnoses: Pyoderma Gangrenosum (PG) associated with systemic neutrophilic dermatosis
PASH syndrome (Pyoderma gangrenosum, Acne, and Suppurative Hidradenitis)
Disseminated staphylococcal or fungal infection (less likely given antibiotic failure)
next_steps: Consultation with Dermatology and Rheumatology for urgent management of suspected neutrophilic dermatosis
Perform a wedge biopsy from the active border of an ulcer for histopathological examination
Initiate high-dose systemic glucocorticoids (e.g., prednisone or methylprednisolone) as the primary treatment
Order a colonoscopy to screen for occult Crohn's disease, given family history and clinical presentation
Discontinue unnecessary broad-spectrum antibiotics to reduce pathergy risk and further diagnostic confusion
Obtain specialized laboratory testing for ANA, ANCA, complement levels, and serum protein electrophoresis
Avoid unnecessary surgical or invasive procedures due to the high risk of pathergy
